stemHtml: Receiver Autonomous Integrity Monitoring (RAIM) is a method used to ensure GNSS position integrity within a GNSS receiver. This is achieved by using the 3D position and timing data from...

options.a: a database in the GPS receiver and compare to the 3D position and time data derived from 4 satellites.
options.b: more than one GPS receiver and compare these to eliminate the receiver that shows incorrect data.
options.c: multiple aircraft systems and compare to the GPS 3D position and time data to check for consistency.
options.d: any number of visible satellites beyond 4 to create several independent positions which should agree.
